issues_found: PAGE_TYPE violation: source is a listing_page per source metadata, which CORE RULE 6 explicitly requires rejection
Source has no archival value as a standalone document - it is a navigation index containing links to dozens of separate press releases
event_type incorrectly set to 'unknown' - listing pages with mixed content do not have a single event type
source.published_at shows '2020-01-01' which is inaccurate - this is a year-spanning listing page with items dated throughout 2020 (earliest seen 10/7/2020, latest 12/29/2020)
No individual macro catalyst or market structure content can be extracted from this index format
The summary and key_points correctly identify the page as non-archiveable, but the record was still submitted for archival rather than properly rejected at intake
Historical age (2020 vs TODAY 2026-03-31) further compounds lack of current relevance
human_review_reason: The issues are clear-cut: this is explicitly a listing_page per source metadata, which CORE RULE 6 mandates rejection. The generated record itself acknowledges this in its summary and notes. No human review needed - the decision follows directly from the stated rules.
